name: India — tenancy & labour (illustrative subset)
disclaimer: Illustrative rules for a demo. Not legal advice. Verify against current law and consult legal aid.

rules.citation: Model Tenancy Act, 2021 — security deposit must be returned minus lawful deductions
rules.plain_reason: A landlord cannot keep your full security deposit for any reason; it must be refunded minus genuine, itemised damages.

rules.citation: Model Tenancy Act, 2021 — deposit typically capped at 2 months (residential)
rules.plain_reason: Security deposit for a home is normally capped at about two months' rent; anything far above that is excessive.

rules.citation: Tenancy law — minimum written notice required before eviction
rules.plain_reason: A landlord must give proper written notice (commonly 15 days to a month) before asking you to leave — not 24–48 hours.

rules.citation: Payment of Wages Act, 1936 — earned wages must be paid
rules.plain_reason: Wages you have already earned cannot be withheld or forfeited; they must be paid by the due date.

rules.citation: Indian Contract Act, 1872 §74 — only reasonable compensation is enforceable
rules.plain_reason: A penalty must be a genuine, reasonable estimate of loss; large punitive penalties are usually not enforceable.

keywords: may change these terms at any time
rules.citation: Consumer Protection Act, 2019 — unilateral one-sided terms may be 'unfair contracts'
rules.plain_reason: One side being able to change the rules anytime, without telling you, is a one-sided unfair term.

keywords: payable in advance on or before
rules.plain_reason: A clear, standard rent-payment schedule is normal and fair.
